prompt: Explain how a full indexing run moves from the CLI into runtime orchestration, file discovery, symbol extraction, persistence, and search or snapshot refresh. Cite the files that support the answer.

expected_symbols.name: run_index
expected_symbols.why: CLI entry for the index command.

expected_symbols.name: WorkspaceManifest::build_execution_plan
expected_symbols.why: Builds the refresh plan used during indexing.

expected_symbols.name: WorkspaceIndexer::run
expected_symbols.why: Runs source indexing over workspace files.

expected_symbols.name: index_file
expected_symbols.why: Extracts file-level nodes, edges, and occurrences.

expected_symbols.name: Storage::flush_projection_batch
expected_symbols.why: Persists graph projection batches produced by indexing.

expected_symbols.name: Storage::rebuild_search_symbol_projection_from_node_table
expected_symbols.why: Refreshes the search projection from persisted graph data.

expected_symbols.name: SnapshotStore::refresh_all_with_stats
expected_symbols.why: Refreshes summary and detail snapshots after data changes.

expected_claims.text: The CLI index command prepares command options and delegates indexing work into the runtime layer.
The runtime opens the workspace and store, chooses full or incremental indexing, and coordinates later refresh phases.
The workspace crate is responsible for source-file discovery and refresh-plan construction.
The indexer extracts nodes, edges, occurrences, and related symbol data from source files.
The store persists graph and file data to SQLite and rebuilds query/search projections from persisted data.
Snapshot refresh happens after persisted data changes so later grounding and summary reads see current indexed state.

forbidden_claims.text: The CLI directly parses source files for normal indexing instead of delegating indexing orchestration.
Indexing results are only kept in memory and are not persisted.
The workspace crate performs symbol extraction from source text.
A remote service is required for a normal full indexing run.
